title: Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.13-14 | 27-09-2016
speaker: HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

segments.text: The general procedure for Vedic marriage is that a father offers his daughter to a suitable boy.
That is a very respectable marriage.
A boy should not go to the girl's father and ask for the hand of his daughter in marriage.
That is considered to be a humbling one's respectable position.
Swaimbhuvamanu wanted to convince Kardvamuni, since he knew that the sage wanted to marry a suitable girl.

I am offering just such a suitable wife. Do not reject the offer, or else, because you are in need of a wife, you will have to ask for such a wife from someone else, who may not behave with you so well.
In that case, your position will be humbled.
Another feature of this incident is that Swaimbhuvamanu,
was the emperor, but he went to offer his qualified daughter to a poor brahmana.
Kardvamuni had no worldly possessions, he was a hermit living in the forest, but he was advanced in culture.
Therefore, in offering one's daughter to a person, the culture and quality are counted as prominent, not wealth or any other material considerations.
I will read the next verse also.
aham tvashenavam vidvan vivahartam samuddhi
adhyatam atastam upakurvanah pratham pratigrahaname
Swaimbhuvamanu continued,
O wise man, I heard that you were prepared to marry. Please accept her hand which is being offered to you by me, since you have not taken a vow of perpetual celibacy.
The principle of brahmacharya is celibacy. There are two kinds of brahmacharis.
One is called naishtika brahmachari,
which means one who takes a vow of celibacy for his whole life,
whereas the other, upakurvanah brahmachari,
or a brahmachari who takes the vow of celibacy up to a certain age.
For example, he may take the vow to remain celibate up to 25 years of age,
then with the permission of his spiritual master, he enters married life.
Brahmacharya is student life,
the beginning of life in the spiritual orders,
so brahmacharya is celibacy.
Only a householder can indulge in sense gratification or sex life, not a brahmachari.
Swaimbhuvamanu requested Garudvamuni to accept his daughter,
and since Garudvama had not taken the vow of naishtika brahmachari,
he was willing to marry the suitable daughter of a high royal family,
was presented.
He was willing to marry, and the suitable daughter of a high royal family was presented.
So Swaimbhuvamanu is requesting Garudvamuni with various reasons,
why he should accept his offer,
the offer of giving his daughter in marriage to Garudvamuni.
So various reasons are being quoted by Swaimbhuvamanu.
Different logic is being put forth to Garudvamuni.
And all these logic, they are all just like one who rejects an offering that comes of its own accord,
but later begs a boon from a miser, loses his widespread reputation,
and his pride is humbled by the neglectful behavior of others.
These are the intricacies of the laws of karma.
So, not that Garudvamuni has to, you know, get into this wedlock,
because of those reasons, because he was an elevated person,
and he was a muni, a brahmana, a hermit living in the forest,
and Prabhupada says that he was advanced in culture,
so therefore the king was offering his daughter to him.
Somehow or the other, by will of providence, Garudvamuni has also decided to become a grahastha.
So there is nothing absolute like, you know, one should not become grahastha, one should remain brahmacharya, etc.
There is nothing absolute as far as the material condition of the living entity is concerned.
He can be in any varna, he can be in any ashrama,
but it is up to the person to make a decision how he should, what ashrama he should choose, etc.
He should have good reasons.
Reasons means with knowledge.
Not social reasons, not prestige reasons.
Oh, I will be a brahmacharya, I will be a sannyasi, that will be great.
Not all those things, for one's own internal reasoning.
Not for the society thing, for his own internal reasoning.
He may decide either to become grahastha or not become grahastha, remain a brahmacharya.
If one is very serious about chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra,
serious about spiritual life, seriously dedicated to Prabhupada's mission,
then Prabhupada himself set the precedence of admitting grahasthas into the ashramas.
Unlike Gaudiya Math, where no grahasthas were admitted into the ashramas.
So he created a system and Prabhupada said that if you can live like a brahmana,
husband and wife, then you can leave off the temple.
But if you want to live like a regular grahastha,
have all the, you know, what the shastra says, how a regular grahastha should be,
then he should go out, earn money and be that.
That also does not stop.
If the person is chanting Hare Krishna, that also does not stop.
But these are all uphill tasks.
To be in the midst of opulence, to be in the midst of acquiring so many things,
definitely the quality of one's chanting, quality of one's advancement will come down.
So devotees should, whoever has taken this path of Krishna consciousness should understand
that it is a path of ultimately catering to the minimum needs of the body and the mind,
whether one is a brahmacharya, grahastha, whatever it is.
That is the principle.
The principle is not to acquire things, not to create possessions.
Not to have a luxurious lifestyle.
These are all the principles, irrespective of what the ashrama is.
Because once a devotee has accepted the path of Krishna consciousness, what is his decision?
His decision is maintain my body and soul, my society, connections, all these things to the minimum.
Certain things are required, I should maintain those minimum things.
And where is my expansion? My expansion is internal.
My expansion is to move forward in Krishna consciousness.
My expansion is in understanding transcendental knowledge.
My expansion is in understanding very clearly difference between matter and spirit.
And after understanding, I can move forward.
